 {"id":"bd-31yg8","title":"Robot outputs: migrate to standard RobotResponse envelope","description":"Migrate non-compliant robot output types to embed RobotResponse (success/timestamp/error/hint). See internal/robot/envelope_test.go list (CASS outputs, Status/Plan/Snapshot/Graph/Alerts/Recipes/Triage, Ack/Spawn/Health/SessionHealth/Interrupt/Dashboard). Ensure arrays are non-nil, timestamps are RFC3339 UTC, and update unit + E2E tests where schema changes require.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedBridge","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:12:53.401951655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:55:34.376690358Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:55:34.376672094Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31yg8","depends_on_id":"bd-3045p","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31yvp","title":"EPIC: Web Platform Test Suite","description":"## Summary\nComprehensive tests for the web platform with detailed logging: unit, contract, parity, integration, WebSocket, UI E2E, and load tests.\n\n## Scope\n- Unit tests for kernel/services + REST handlers.\n- OpenAPI contract tests + schema stability.\n- Parity tests: CLI vs REST output equivalence.\n- Integration tests: API + tmux + tool bridges.\n- WebSocket tests: resume/backpressure/fan‑out.\n- UI E2E tests: key user flows (sessions, send, beads, mail).\n- Load tests: WS concurrency and pane streaming.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Tests cover all major user workflows with explicit logging.\n- CI runs parity + contract tests on every change.\n- E2E suites are deterministic and produce logs on failure.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:48:54.904617858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:03:04.894729268Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-19jj7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-1zk1v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-2gjig","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-2nrkq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qq5u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-3a2ry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31yvp","depends_on_id":"bd-3ef1l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Feature: Extend checkpoints with assignments/progress","description":"## Overview
Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkpoints remain readable even without new fields
- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled
- Restore flow surfaces assignment summary","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:12.436057119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:21.091234924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32na1","title":"Bug: scan notifications ignore ** reservation patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:55:17.133963329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:55:22.290685468Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:55:22.290606531Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-332h","title":"Task: Implement file edit detection patterns","description":"## Purpose
Extend robot/patterns.go with patterns to detect file edits from agent output.

## Detection Patterns per Agent Type
var fileWritePatterns = map[AgentType][]string{
    AgentClaude: {
        'using the edit tool',
        'using the write tool',
        'Edited file:',
        'Created file:',
        'Writing to file:',
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'writing to file',
        'edit.*file',
        'created file',
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'Writing:',
        'Editing:',
        'Created:',
    },
}

## Path Extraction
func ExtractEditedFiles(output string, agentType AgentType) []string

Extract file paths from patterns like:
- 'Edited file: internal/cli/send.go'
- 'Writing to file /path/to/file.go'
- Tool use JSON with file_path field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Patterns detect 90%+ of file edits
- File paths extracted accurately
- Both relative and absolute paths handled
- False positives minimized

## Technical Notes
Use regexp.MustCompile for pattern matching
Test against actual agent output samples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:28.921638062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773414528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773257222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-332h","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33f7m","title":"Task: Implement ntm repo sync wrapper (ru passthrough)","description":"## Overview
Add `ntm repo sync` to call ru with minimal argument translation.

## Behavior
- Accepts ru flags via passthrough (e.g., `--workers`, `--dry-run`)
- Emits clear error if ru not installed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wrapper is thin and predictable
- No additional config format introduced","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:00:42.936235289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:42:37.690483300Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:42:37.690414362Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33f7m","depends_on_id":"bd-2ectj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
